2605.13450.title: Assessing the Creativity of Large Language Models: Testing, Limits, and New Frontiers
2605.13450.paper_summary: Measuring the creativity of large language models (LLMs) is essential for designing methods that can improve creativity and for enhancing our scientific understanding of this ability. To accomplish this, it has become common in recent years to administer tests of human creativity to LLMs. Although these tests provide a convenient and fully automated way to score "creativity," their validity as measures of machine creativity has not been established, and these tests already have limited validity as predictors of human creativity. To address this problem, we conduct the first large-scale, systematic study assessing the effectiveness of human creativity tests for predicting the creative achievement of LLMs across three target constructs: creative writing, divergent thinking, and scientific ideation. We find that the Divergent Association Task (DAT) and the Conditional DAT are the best predictors of creative writing and divergent thinking, respectively, but that test effectiveness varies significantly by construct, and no single test predicts all constructs well. Moreover, contrary to popular belief, no existing test reliably predicts scientific ideation ability. Motivated by this problem, we introduce the Divergent Remote Association Test (DRAT), a vocabulary-space test that assesses both convergent and divergent thinking in a single instrument. The DRAT is the first and only creativity test for LLMs that is a significant predictor of scientific ideation ability, demonstrating robustness across major design choices. Furthermore, the performance gain of the DRAT is not recoverable from any linear combination of the Divergent Association Task and the Remote Associates Test, indicating that assessing divergent and convergent thinking in the same test is essential to reliably predicting scientific ideation ability.
2605.13450.paper_summary_zh: 測量大型語言模型（LLMs）的創造力對於設計能夠提升創造力的方法以及增進我們對這種能力的科學理解至關重要。<lb/>為了達成這一目標，近年來對LLMs進行人類創造力測試已變得相當普遍。<lb/>儘管這些測試提供了一種方便且完全自動化的方式來評分「創造力」，但作為機器創造力的測量工具，其有效性尚未確立，而這些測試作為人類創造力預測工具的有效性也已經受到限制。<lb/>為了解決這一問題，我們進行了首個大規模、系統性的研究，評估人類創造力測試在預測LLMs的創造性成就方面的有效性，涵蓋三個目標構念：創意寫作、發散思維和科學構思。<lb/>我們發現，發散聯想任務（DAT）和條件DAT分別是創意寫作和發散思維的最佳預測指標，但測試的有效性在不同構念之間有顯著差異，且沒有單一測試能夠良好預測所有構念。<lb/>此外，與普遍認知相反，現有的測試並不能可靠地預測科學構思能力。<lb/>受到這一問題的驅動，我們引入了發散遠程聯想測試（DRAT），這是一種詞彙空間測試，能夠在一個工具中評估收斂思維和發散思維。<lb/>DRAT是首個也是唯一一個能夠顯著預測科學構思能力的LLMs創造力測試，並在主要設計選擇上顯示出穩健性。<lb/>此外，DRAT的性能提升無法通過發散聯想任務和遠程聯想測試的任何線性組合來恢復，這表明在同一測試中評估發散思維和收斂思維對於可靠預測科學構思能力至關重要。
2605.13450.author: Samuel Schapiro et.al.
2605.13450.authors: Samuel Schapiro, Alexi Gladstone, Jonah Black, Heng Ji
